claim: For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6.

For any positive prime n, the divisor sum of n is n + 1.
For any prime n greater than 1, the number of divisors of n is 2.
For any prime n greater than 1, the sum of divisor and totient functions is 2n.
predicate: (not is_prime(n) or n <= 1) or (sigma(n) + phi(n) == 2 * n)

claim: For any prime n greater than 1, the exponent of n in the prime factorization of n^3 - n is exactly 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n) or n <= 1) or (vp(n**3 - n, n) == 1)

claim: For all primes n greater than 2, the exponent of 2 in the prime factorization of n^3 - n is at least 3.
For all primes n greater than 1, the exponent of 3 in the prime factorization of n^3 - n is at least 1.
For all primes n greater than 3, the sum of the exponents of 2 and 3 in n^3 - n is at least 4.
predicate: (not is_prime(n) or n <= 3) or (vp(n**3 - n, 2) + vp(n**3 - n, 3) >= 4)

claim: For all integers n greater than 1, the divisor sum of n is strictly greater than its Euler totient.
